# kmp-ble

> The complete BLE toolkit for Kotlin Multiplatform — Android & iOS from one codebase.

## What is kmp-ble?

kmp-ble is a Kotlin Multiplatform Bluetooth Low Energy library. It provides a coroutine-based API for BLE communication across Android (API 33+) and iOS (15+) from shared Kotlin code.

Unlike other KMP BLE libraries that only cover scanning and connecting (GATT client), kmp-ble covers the full BLE stack: GATT client, GATT server, advertising, L2CAP channels, firmware updates (DFU), type-safe GATT profile parsing, and a complete testing framework.

## Installation

Maven Central group: `com.atruedev`

```kotlin
// build.gradle.kts
kotlin {
    sourceSets {
        commonMain.dependencies {
            // Core — scan, connect, GATT client/server, advertise, L2CAP
            implementation("com.atruedev:kmp-ble:0.3.11")

            // Optional modules
            implementation("com.atruedev:kmp-ble-profiles:0.3.11")  // type-safe GATT profiles
            implementation("com.atruedev:kmp-ble-dfu:0.3.11")       // Nordic DFU firmware updates
            implementation("com.atruedev:kmp-ble-codec:0.3.11")     // typed read/write codec
            implementation("com.atruedev:kmp-ble-quirks:0.3.11")    // OEM device workarounds
        }
    }
}
```

Android initialization:

```kotlin
class MyApp : Application() {
    override fun onCreate() {
        super.onCreate()
        KmpBle.init(this)
    }
}
```

iOS via Swift Package Manager:

```
https://github.com/gary-quinn/kmp-ble
```

## Core Features

### Scanning

```kotlin
val scanner = Scanner {
    timeout = 30.seconds
    filters {
        match { serviceUuid(ServiceUuid.HEART_RATE) }
    }
}
scanner.advertisements.collect { ad ->
    println("Found: ${ad.name} rssi=${ad.rssi}")
}
```

### Connect and Read/Write

```kotlin
val peripheral = advertisement.toPeripheral()
peripheral.connect()

val char = peripheral.findCharacteristic(
    serviceUuid = uuidFrom("180d"),
    characteristicUuid = uuidFrom("2a37"),
)!!

val value = peripheral.read(char)
peripheral.write(char, byteArrayOf(0x01), WriteType.WithResponse)

peripheral.observeValues(char).collect { data ->
    println("Heart rate: ${data[1]}")
}
```

### GATT Server (Phone as Peripheral)

```kotlin
val server = GattServer {
    service(uuidFrom("180d")) {
        characteristic(uuidFrom("2a37")) {
            properties(read = true, notify = true)
            onRead { byteArrayOf(0x00, 72) }
        }
    }
}
server.open()
```

### Advertising

```kotlin
val advertiser = Advertiser {
    name("MyDevice")
    serviceUuids(uuidFrom("180d"))
}
advertiser.start()
```

### Nordic DFU (Firmware Updates)

```kotlin
val controller = DfuController(peripheral)
controller.performDfu(firmware).collect { progress ->
    when (progress) {
        is DfuProgress.Transferring -> println("${progress.fraction * 100}%")
        is DfuProgress.Complete -> println("DFU done")
        else -> {}
    }
}
```

### Type-safe Profiles

```kotlin
// Instead of parsing raw bytes:
peripheral.heartRateMeasurements().collect { measurement ->
    println("BPM: ${measurement.heartRate}")
    println("Contact: ${measurement.contactDetected}")
}

val battery = peripheral.readBatteryLevel() // returns Int? (0-100)
val deviceInfo = peripheral.readDeviceInformation() // returns DeviceInformation data class
```

Available profiles: Heart Rate (0x180D), Battery (0x180F), Device Information (0x180A), Blood Pressure (0x1810), Glucose (0x1808), Cycling Speed & Cadence (0x1816).

### Typed Codec

```kotlin
// Define a decoder
val myDecoder = BleDecoder<Int> { bytes -> bytes[1].toInt() }

// Typed read instead of raw ByteArray
val heartRate: Int = peripheral.read(char, myDecoder)

// Typed observe
peripheral.observeValues(char, myDecoder).collect { value ->
    println("Value: $value")
}
```

### Testing Without Hardware

```kotlin
val peripheral = FakePeripheral {
    service("180d") {
        characteristic("2a37") {
            properties(read = true, write = true, notify = true)
            onRead { byteArrayOf(0x00, 72) }
            onWrite { data, writeType -> /* verify written data */ }
            respondAfter(200.milliseconds)  // simulate BLE latency
        }
        characteristic("2a39") {
            failWith(GattError(WriteNotPermitted))  // simulate errors
        }
    }
}

val scanner = FakeScanner {
    advertisement {
        name("HeartSensor")
        rssi(-55)
        serviceUuids("180d")
    }
}
```

Also available: FakeGattServer, FakeAdvertiser, FakeL2capChannel.

### Bonding and Reconnection

```kotlin
peripheral.connect(ConnectionOptions(
    bondingPreference = BondingPreference.Required,
    reconnectionStrategy = ReconnectionStrategy.ExponentialBackoff(
        initialDelay = 1.seconds,
        maxDelay = 30.seconds,
        maxAttempts = 10,
    )
))
```

### L2CAP Channels

High-throughput streaming bypassing GATT for bulk data transfer and DFU.

### Device Quirks

Built-in workarounds for BLE bugs on Samsung, Pixel, Xiaomi, OnePlus devices via the quirks module.

## Architecture

- 14-state connection state machine with exhaustive transition table
- Per-peripheral serial execution via limitedParallelism(1) — no locks
- Flow-based reactive APIs throughout
- Zero-copy BleData on iOS (wraps NSData)
- 460+ tests, CI on every push

## Key Differences from Other KMP BLE Libraries

- Full peripheral role: GATT server + advertiser (not just client)
- Built-in DFU with Nordic Secure DFU v2
- Type-safe GATT profile parsers (6 standard profiles)
- Testing framework with delay/error injection
- OEM device quirks registry
- L2CAP channel support
- BLE 5.0 extended advertising

## Links

- GitHub: https://github.com/gary-quinn/kmp-ble
- API Docs: https://gary-quinn.github.io/kmp-ble/
- Maven Central: https://central.sonatype.com/artifact/com.atruedev/kmp-ble
